titleStmt.title: Letter from Dorothy Duffin to Maria Duffin, 28 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Dorothy Duffin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is part of a rich correspondence between various members of the Duffin family, a large prosperous family living in Strandtown, Belfast. Several family members served in the war, including Major Terence Duffin, who served as a staff officer with 107 Brigade, and later with Royal Irish Rifles; Major Charles G Duffin MC, Royal Field Artillery; and their sisters Emma, Celia and Dorothy who served as Voluntary Aid Detachments (VADS) in Egypt and France, and with the YMCA.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Sunday Nov 28th 1915 No. 16 General
Hospital
S.P.O. V13
Dear Mamma I was glad to see
your letter this morning. I think
Lucie must have forgotten to post
the one I wrote you last Sunday
or you would have got it before
24th (the Oric on John). Ms Bell
will not be back till the end
of this week or the beginning of
next (4th-6th) so I shall more
likely stay on till then and then go
to Eastbourne. I shall let you
know when I arrive there. The
cold here is frightful, everything
frozen hard. The water freezing
in the jugs in the house! I have
been playing golf nearly every
day but this frost will stop it. This plain Higgins may have head
off the Home enough from the Cavlers
who know them in the Isle of
Wight. For we lack a litte
science but I am One at the
counter now. Best love to
all. Dorothy
